description: A body 'tupi' when it pushes away from a supporting surface and passes briefly through the air. The jump may go upward, downward, or across a gap. 'tupi' follows the launch and airborne interval; 'kamo' (arrive) can give the landing its own clause. 'lepa' (fall) reports unsupported descent without the propulsive launch.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't', release it, and round the lips while raising the back of the tongue for stressed high-back 'u'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p', then release them into high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The first stop releases into deep rounded 'u', then the lips close and spring apart into bright 'i'. The compact shift from gathered pressure to a lighter ending can recall a body leaving the ground.
usage_notes: A source can take 'lue' (ABL), and a reached landing place takes 'kau' (ALL).

examples.translation: The rabbit jumps in the grass.

semantic_domains.activity: a jump treated as one bounded bodily act
semantic_domains.physical: the body's push away from a supporting surface
semantic_domains.spatial: a brief airborne path between positions
